mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in retail and food services sales (RSAFS) reflects robust consumer demand which drives corporate earnings higher, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: If the rank correlation between RSAFS level changes and subsequent 20-day SPX returns is non-positive over any 3-year rolling window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 8%, it signals economic distress and declining consumer confidence, which depresses equity valuations and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If in the 12 months following any trigger activation, the proportion of positive 20-day SPX returns exceeds 50%, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in industrial production (INDPRO) and nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) indicate broad-based economic expansion, raising earnings expectations and stock prices within the next week.
failure_condition: If the mean 5-day SPX return following simultaneous month-over-month increases in INDPRO and PAYEMS is not significantly positive (t-test p>0.05) over the test period, reject.

mechanism_narrative: In a high unemployment environment, a rise in housing starts (HOUST) signals an incipient housing market recovery, which acts as a leading indicator of broader economic improvement and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: If the Spearman correlation between HOUST changes and subsequent 60-day SPX returns is non-positive during periods of UNRATE > 8% over a 5-year window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A year-over-year increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL) above 3% heightens expectations of monetary tightening, which compresses valuations and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: If the average 5-day SPX return after a CPIAUCSL YoY > 3% trigger is not significantly negative (t-test p>0.05) over a 10-year period, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales (RSAFS) increase concurrently with a decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE), it reflects strengthening consumer sector and labor market, boosting investor confidence and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: If the mean 20-day SPX return following months with both RSAFS MoM > 0 and UNRATE MoM < 0 is not significantly positive (t-test p>0.05) or if hit rate is below 55%, reject.

mechanism_narrative: During the early recovery phase of the business cycle, accelerating growth in industrial production (INDPRO) is a reliable signal of strengthening economic momentum, which boosts corporate profits and leads to positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
regime_hint: early recovery phase (post-recession, UNRATE declining from peak)
failure_condition: If the rank IC between INDPRO growth and 60-day SPX returns is ≤0 in the 2 years following the start of a recovery as defined by NBER turning points, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A rising path of real gross domestic product (GDPC1) signifies robust aggregate demand and corporate earnings growth, directly supporting higher S&P 500 equity valuations over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If the one-quarter-ahead annualized SPX return following a positive GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth is not positive on average over a 10-year period, reject.
